user_prompt: Please implement the code as described by the comment on src/tests/test_speaker.cpp:1
make sure the built-in speaker is initialized
stuff the `g_fifo_bt2spk` FIFO with 500 milliseconds of a square wave tone at 440 Hz and then a 500ms of 880 Hz square wave.
have messages printed out via Serial
Replace the comment with your implementation.
Optimize for speed. Don't over-explore or over-check your work other than what is absolutely necessary to implement the comment.
Once you are done, remove the comment.
agent_reply: Implemented [src/tests/test_speaker.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_speaker.cpp) with a `test_speaker()` smoke test that:
- Queues 500 ms of 440 Hz square wave, then 500 ms of 880 Hz

user_prompt: hmmm all the messages came through, I didn't hear anything
none of the messages mention I2S
agent_reply: I made two fixes aimed at exactly that “messages but no sound” symptom.
In [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp), the Core2 speaker enable path now follows M5Unified’s behavior:
- Configures the NS4168 I2S mono slot mask as `I2S_STD_SLOT_BOTH`, matching M5Unified’s speaker setup more closely
In [test_speaker.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_speaker.cpp), I added Serial-visible I2S messages like:
enabling I2S TX: port=0 rate=16000 Hz BCLK=12 LRCK=0 DOUT=2 mono slot=BOTH
pumping FIFO to I2S speaker output
I also changed the final pump stage so it keeps pumping I2S for a short settle window instead of trusting FIFO emptiness alone.

In [test_speaker.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_speaker.cpp), I added Serial-visible I2S messages like:

commit_message: got a physical unit, starting bringup tests

user_prompt: move literally all insstances of idle_forever into `lib\UtilFuncs\utilfuncs.cpp`, it doesn't need to be fancy
agent_reply: Done. `idle_forever()` now lives in [utilfuncs.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.cpp) with its declaration in [utilfuncs.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/UtilFuncs/utilfuncs.h), and the local copies were removed from all the test files.
It’s intentionally plain now: just loops forever with `delay(1000)`.

commit_message: clean up some tests a bit

user_prompt: look at existing tests, do we have a plain "play out a sine wave" test already?
agent_reply: No, I don’t see a plain “play a sine wave” test already.
Closest is [test_speaker.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_speaker.cpp:34), but it generates a **square wave**, not sine:
- targets the built-in speaker path via `AudioManager::Hardware::M5StackInternal`

So we already have the skeleton for “generate PCM and push it out,” but not a clean sine-wave output test. For validating SGTL5000/LEDC MCLK, that square wave is also a harsher signal spectrally, but a sine test would be better for listening for pitch wobble, dropouts, or distortion.
